exemplar.title: SysTune - an app that would automatically optimize the system through the usage of buttons and switches, allowing complex changes in the system without having to search and change each setting manually.

detailedExemplarData.title: SysTune - an app that would automatically optimize the system through the usage of buttons and switches, allowing complex changes in the system without having to search and change each setting manually.

strengths: Detailed evidence of multiple client consultations demonstrating real needs
Fully justified product rationale linking every feature to client usability
Comprehensive and measurable success criteria aligned to client requirements
weaknesses: Interview findings are referenced in an appendix rather than briefly summarized in the main rationale
A few successâcriteria phrasings could be more specific (e.g., exact UI controls)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The planning section meets the 5â6 band across all strands: the scenario and client investigation are thorough, the rationale is fully justified, and the nine success criteria are specific and measurable. Minor suggestions do not detract from topâband performance, so a full 6 is awarded.

strengths: High complexity integrating GUI, registry edits, hardware monitoring, and automation
Appropriate and effective use of specialized tools (PyQt Designer, PyInstaller, psutil, etc.)
Comprehensive source attribution with clear explanation of techniques
weaknesses: Some inline code and doc comments lack specific attributions to source inspiration
Minor inefficiencies (manual batch scripting) and limited discussion of edge-case exception handling
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Complexity and tool usage are both in the top band, and documentation nearly reaches full marks. Only small gaps in attribution and build automation keep it just below perfection, justifying an 11/12.

strengths: Comprehensive design overview with detailed UML, structure charts, and flowcharts
Thorough test plan linking each test to success criteria, procedure, and expected outcome
weaknesses: No dedicated record-of-tasks table, falling to band 0 for that strand
Minor formatting issues in test plan and repeated similar test cases
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the design overview and test plan are nearâtop band, the complete absence of a chronological record of tasks prevents a higher score. The balanced performance across the three strands places this criterion solidly in the 3â4 band, meriting a 4.

strengths: All core features demonstrably function well with graceful error handling
Modular design with JSONâserialized profiles and encapsulated command lists allows straightforward extension
weaknesses: Occasional hardware detection failures acknowledged but not fully eliminated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The product operates reliably across its full feature set and the design supports straightforward modification. Although portability is somewhat restricted, both strands attain topâband descriptors, warranting the maximum 4/4.

strengths: Systematic evaluation against all nine success criteria with balanced evidence
Realistic, wellâjustified future recommendations aligned to client feedback
Summary of multiple client interview comments demonstrates authentic feedback
weaknesses: Reliance on anecdotal rather than measured performance data
Client quotations are not embedded in the main body
Recommendations are not prioritized by impact or effort
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation and recommendations meet the descriptors for band 5. The lack of quantitative benchmarks and embedded client quotes prevents a perfect score, so a 5/6 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The solution overview contains a UML class diagram, layered structure charts, detailed flowcharts for every major algorithm, and annotated screen mock-ups.  Relationships between components and data flow are explicit and coherent, demonstrating a âcomprehensive design overviewâ that clearly informs development.

The rationale explains language (Python), framework (PyQt), file format (.cfg) and feature set in direct relation to the clientâs inexperience and performance needs.  Every major design choice is tied back to usability, safety or portability, meeting the requirement for a âfully-justifiedâ rationale.  Minor gaps (e.g. brief mention of speed not being a concern) keep it short of an unquestionable 6.

The student leverages PyQt Designer, PyInstaller, ctypes, winreg, psutil, GPUtil and batch scripting effectively.  Evidence shows drag-and-drop UI generation, automated .exe builds and systematic exception handling, all illustrating âappropriate and effective useâ across several specialised tools.  Small inefficiencies (e.g., manual .bat creation) keep the score just below the maximum.

The tabular test plan links each test to a specific success criterion, goal, procedure and expected outcome.  Coverage spans dialog logic, profile import/export, registry edits, command execution, stats widget and exception handling.  A few descriptions are word-wrapped awkwardly but the content is complete, meriting the top band, albeit at 5 due to minor formatting issues.

Recommendations address hardware-detection improvements, UX refinements, external documentation links, extended settings and benchmarkingâall realistic and aligned with client feedback.  Each is briefly justified, meeting the descriptor for ârealistic and well-justifiedâ suggestions, with slight brevity keeping it at 5.

A full reference list cites all external software and libraries; screenshots and code snippets credit Qt Designer and PSUtil, and the narrative explains how each library is employed.  While inline code comments are present, not every snippet attributes source inspiration, so the strand earns the lower end of the top band.

No chronological record-of-tasks table (date, task, evidence, comment) is provided.  Narrative descriptions of tools and development steps do not meet the rubricâs requirement for a dedicated record, so the strand falls in band 0.

The product integrates GUI construction, registry manipulation, live hardware monitoring, privilege-elevated command execution, profile serialisation and build automation.  This combination shows more than moderate complexity, though algorithms remain procedural and domain-specific rather than deeply algorithmic, fitting solidly within the 5-8 to 9-12 boundary; a score of 10 reflects strong but not extraordinary ingenuity.

Nine success criteria cover interface safety, system feedback, functional breadth, error handling and security.  Each criterion is specific, measurable within the test plan and clearly appropriate to the problem, fulfilling the requirement for a âcomprehensive range of appropriate criteriaâ.

The student cites seven interviews and summarises client comments on UI clarity, missing stats and info depth.  While direct quotations/screenshots are not embedded in the main body, the references to Appendix pages indicate authentic, iterative feedback, fulfilling âsome comprehensive feedback,â hence upper-middle band.

Profiles are JSON-serialised, settings are modular and new commands can be added as list items, suggesting straightforward expansion.  Registry paths and command lists are encapsulated in functions, but tight Windows-specific coupling limits portability, so a 3 within the top band is justified.

The student clearly identifies a real client (a friend), describes the clientâs specific performance-related frustrations and links them to the Windows optimisation context.  Multiple consultations (WhatsApp interview, five additional interviews referenced in the appendix) are cited, demonstrating ongoing dialogue.  This satisfies the descriptor of âdetailed evidence of consultationâ and fully justifies the top band.

The demonstration script and narrative indicate that all core featuresâdialog safety check, settings toggles, import/export, stats panel and system commandsâoperate as intended.  Occasional detection failures are acknowledged but handled gracefully, aligning with âproduct functions wellâ.

The evaluation revisits each of the nine success criteria, specifying whether it was fully or partially met and referencing observed behaviour.  Discussion is balanced and evidence-based, though some judgements (e.g., performance boost) rely on anecdotal rather than measured data, warranting 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The scenario description demonstrates detailed evidence of consultation with a real client and clearly articulates their performance frustrations, satisfying the top band for client investigation.
The product rationale links each feature choice back to client needs and usability concerns, fully justifying your technology and UI design decisions.

Consider integrating a brief summary of key interview findings directly into the rationale section rather than relying solely on footnote references to the appendix; this improves read-through and traceability.
The nine success criteria are specific, measurable and directly aligned to client needs and the appâs functionality, demonstrating comprehensive planning.
The phrasing âcould be simplified with simple âbuttonsââ is vague. Specify exact UI controls or workflows (e.g., toggle switches with tooltips) to clarify how complexity is hidden.
Remove administrative details like the wordâcount line from the main content; they detract from the academic narrative and belong in a cover sheet or appendix.
The UML diagram is comprehensive, but adding labels for class responsibilities and drawing explicit associations between classes would enhance clarity on data flow.
The statement that âPython isnât intended to be used as an OOPâ is inaccurate. Python fully supports object-oriented programmingâconsider revising to reflect its capabilities.
Including a high-level component interaction diagram in addition to structure charts would give a clearer picture of runtime communication between modules.
The project folder structure diagram is clear; adding a legend or brief notes on naming conventions would help a reader unfamiliar with your filesystem organization.
In the structure orientation chart, consider adding arrowheads or labels to indicate data/control flow direction between windows and functions.
Each figure caption should include a figure number (e.g., âFigure 2â) to improve cross-referencing in the narrative.
The flowchart of the terms and conditions window is detailed; labeling each decision node with conditions (e.g., Cancel vs. Yes) would further clarify the logic paths.
Group flowchart steps into swimlanes for user actions versus system processes to make distinct responsibilities explicit.
Your test plan links tests to success criteria nicely; adding quantitative thresholds (e.g., max launch time, error-rate tolerances) will improve measurability.
The first testâs description is overly broad. Defining exact input sequences or failure modes would strengthen coverage and reproducibility.
There are repeated descriptions of import/export tests; consolidating similar cases would reduce redundancy and keep the plan concise.
Mentioning specific VS Code extensions (e.g., linting, debugger) would provide greater transparency into your development environment.
The exhaustive list of QtDesigner widgets is informative but visually overwhelming; focus on the subset you actually used and describe their roles.
In the dialog window code snippet, highlighting how QDialogButtonBox standardizes accept/reject behavior would reinforce best practice usage.
The dialog logic flowchart is clear; adding alternative flows (e.g., auto-cancel on timeout) would demonstrate consideration of additional user scenarios.
Comparing initial and final designs in Figure 12 is useful; explicitly calling out the key changes in the caption or narrative would highlight your iterative process.
Discussing potential security implications of registry edits would demonstrate critical awareness of system-level risks.
The scanner function rationale is strong; suggesting basic benchmarks (e.g., scan time) would help evaluate its performance impact.
The evaluation against success criteria is methodical; however, many judgments are anecdotalâconsider including measured data (e.g., CPU benchmarks) for stronger evidence.
Embedding direct client quotes in the main evaluation section would strengthen the authenticity of feedback evidence.
The QtDesigner UI generation workflow is well documented; annotating the key PySide-Qt code integration would strengthen understanding of the toolchain.
The description of changing system settings lacks mention of edge-case handling (e.g., insufficient permissions). Including these would illustrate deeper complexity management.
The stats retrieval flowchart covers exception handling; discussing polling interval trade-offs (performance vs. freshness) would deepen the development narrative.
Clarify which specific exceptions are caught when components arenât detected to provide precise documentation of error-handling routines.
Your recommendations are well-justified and realistic. Prioritizing them by impact or implementation effort would help in planning subsequent development phases.
Criterion 7 is well covered; consider quantifying âlightweightâ by providing average file size or load times to substantiate the claim.
